specification.topic-statement: The finish reason mapping system translates provider-specific stop reason strings from each provider's streaming or response format into a unified internal finish reason enumeration.

scope.in-scope: Anthropic stop_reason string mapping, OpenAI Chat Completions finish_reason string mapping, and OpenAI Responses API status and incomplete_details mapping.
scope.boundary: How streaming events are normalized across providers is covered by the provider serialization specification. This topic covers only the finish reason string-to-enum translation step within that process.
Google Gemini finish reason mapping is covered by the Google streaming events specification.

field.name: LENGTH
field.note: stopped due to output token limit

field.name: ERROR
field.note: stopped due to an error condition

behavior.anthropic-mapping: Anthropic stop_reason strings map as follows: - "end_turn" → STOP - "max_tokens" → LENGTH - "tool_use" → TOOL_USE - "stop_sequence" → STOP - "refusal" → CONTENT_FILTER - All other values → UNKNOWN
behavior.openai-chat-mapping: OpenAI Chat Completions finish_reason strings map as follows: - "stop" → STOP - "length" → LENGTH - "tool_calls" → TOOL_USE - "content_filter" → CONTENT_FILTER - "error" → ERROR - All other values (including absent) → UNKNOWN
behavior.openai-responses-mapping:
OpenAI Responses API uses a status field with an optional incomplete_details.reason field for incomplete responses.
Primary status mappings: - "completed" → STOP - "failed" → ERROR - "cancelled" → STOP - "incomplete" with incomplete_details.reason "max_output_tokens" → LENGTH - "incomplete" with incomplete_details.reason "content_filter" → CONTENT_FILTER - "incomplete" with any other or absent reason → LENGTH (default for incomplete) - All other status values → UNKNOWN
When the status is "incomplete" but no incomplete_details.reason is provided or the reason is unrecognized, the finish reason defaults to LENGTH rather than UNKNOWN. This is a conservative assumption that the output was cut off by a token limit.
